maneh: A casual way to say 'you' in Sundanese, used only with friends of the same age. Avoid using it with older people as it can be considered rude.
aing: A very casual and blunt way to say 'I' or 'me' in Sundanese. Often used among close male friends. It's very informal and can sound aggressive if used in the wrong context.
mabar: Short for 'Main Bareng' (Play Together). Usually refers to playing online games together.
gercep: Short for 'Gerak Cepat' (Move Fast). Used to describe someone who is very responsive or acts quickly.
gc: The very shortened version for 'Gerak Cepat'
tara: A casual way to say rarely or jarang
ci: Another variation of cai or water and air in sundanese
te: A really shortened version of hente or tidak in sundanese
